readerTask: Explain the same RBAC-versus-Unix-isolation distinction first to an engineer and then to a non-engineering operations reader, without changing the security conclusion.
requiredFacts: Unix isolation controls operating-system execution/filesystem identity.
Enabling one does not automatically provide the other.
inferences: Vocabulary and examples should adapt to the audience.
limitations: Audience adaptation cannot soften the security boundary.
prohibitedOverclaims: The nontechnical explanation may omit the independent layers.

questions.a: What a user may do through the application.
Which OS identity, files, and credentials execution can reach.

questions.q: Did the conclusion change between audiences?
questions.a: No; only vocabulary and example selection changed.
